behaviors.description: Reviewer dispatch sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "sends the same source document and correct reviewer-specific instruction pack to each reviewer role." Slot B: handoff across dispatcher -> reviewer client modules. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a cross-module wiring bug (e.g., swapped instruction pack by role) can be missed even when local builders pass. Rejection-gate: System is disqualified because behavior text does not require real OS/network/subprocess; Agentic is disqualified because it checks invocation payload correctness, not model judgment. Final label: Integration due to Slot B handoff risk and Slot C wiring miss.
behaviors.test_description: Wire dispatcher with in-process reviewer-client fakes that capture invocations for all three roles; assert identical document payload/hash for each call and role-specific instruction pack correctness per role.

behaviors.description: The live clause extractor identifies key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live clause extractor identifies key terms ... present in the contract." Slot B: model-output dependence (extraction quality). Slot C: if lowered to Integration with stubs, tests can pass while the real model misses obligations/penalties in real phrasing. Rejection-gate: Workflow is disqualified because this is a single reviewer decision, not a multi-step agent pipeline. Final label: Agentic because Slot B is decisive and Slot C is a real hidden failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause extractor on a labeled contract set and assert key-term extraction quality (coverage/precision for parties, dates, obligations, penalties) against ground truth thresholds.

behaviors.description: Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Risk-policy configuration is passed correctly to the risk assessor invocation." Slot B: module handoff (config mapping/serialization into invocation). Slot C: if lowered to Unit, a mismatch between config assembler and invocation client contract can be missed. Rejection-gate: Agentic is disqualified by behavior text focusing on passing config, not assessing output quality; System is disqualified because in-process wiring tests can reproduce this failure boundary. Final label: Integration because the failure lives at inter-module handoff (Slot B) and would evade single-function tests (Slot C).
behaviors.test_description: Execute service-layer invocation path with fake risk-assessor client; assert the outbound request contains the exact risk-policy fields, defaults, and overrides expected for the run context.

behaviors.description: The live risk assessor flags language that is problematic under policy, including indirect or euphemistic phrasing.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live risk assessor flags language ... including indirect or euphemistic phrasing." Slot B: model-output quality on nuanced semantic judgment. Slot C: if lowered to Integration with deterministic stubs, euphemism-detection failures in the real model remain invisible. Rejection-gate: Workflow is disqualified because behavior concerns one reviewer’s decision quality, not multiple agentic stages. Final label: Agentic from Slot B, with Slot C showing why lower levels under-detect real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real risk assessor on contracts containing direct and euphemistic risky clauses; evaluate detection recall/precision per policy class and require minimum threshold on euphemistic cases.

behaviors.description: Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "Jurisdiction and regulatory profile are passed correctly to the compliance checker invocation." Slot B: handoff/data-contract boundary between orchestrator and checker client. Slot C: if lowered to Unit, role/context mapping bugs (wrong jurisdiction/profile pair) across modules can be missed. Rejection-gate: Agentic is disqualified because this behavior validates parameter passing, not compliance decision quality; System is disqualified because no real OS/network behavior is required to expose mapping errors. Final label: Integration based on Slot B handoff and Slot C miss risk.
behaviors.test_description: Run orchestration path with an in-process compliance-checker stub that records request payload; assert exact jurisdiction/regulatory profile values and precedence rules are preserved.

behaviors.description: The live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "live compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all configured regulatory requirements." Slot B: model-output dependence (legal/regulatory judgment correctness). Slot C: if lowered to Integration with canned responses, incorrect real-model pass/fail determinations are not detected. Rejection-gate: Workflow is disqualified because this behavior targets one checker’s decision, not chained multi-agent decisions. Final label: Agentic due to Slot B and the concrete hidden failure in Slot C.
behaviors.test_description: Evaluate the real compliance checker on a benchmark set with known regulatory outcomes across profiles; assert pass/fail correctness and requirement-level error rates stay within thresholds.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments, the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is deterministic merge-rule logic at one local boundary: preserving both conflicting assessments plus attribution for the same clause. Rejection-gate: Integration considered but disqualified by behavior text focusing on merge-step rule itself, not cross-module transport/IO; Agentic/System disqualified because no real model judgment or OS/network/subprocess is required.
behaviors.test_description: Unit-test the merge function with two conflicting inputs for the same clause and assert output contains both assessments, correct reviewer attribution, and no overwrite.

behaviors.description: End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) succeeds on a real contract without losing any reviewer findings.
behaviors.justification: Slot A: "End-to-end orchestration (dispatch all three reviewers, collect outputs, merge unified report) ... on a real contract" and "without losing any reviewer findings." Slot B: multi-step agentic pipeline (three real reviewer model calls + orchestration/merge). Slot C: if lowered to Agentic (single-step), concurrency/collection bugs that drop one reviewer’s findings during aggregation are missed. Final label: Workflow because Slot B requires sequenced multi-agent execution and Slot C is a pipeline-level failure.
behaviors.test_description: Run the full pipeline on real contracts: invoke all three live reviewers in parallel, persist each raw output, then merge; assert merged report retains every reviewer finding with attribution and no reviewer output is dropped.
